description: Baseline with autoresearch harness; build dominates presorted first render while visible-first is negligible.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a trustworthy baseline and commit the autoresearch harness before making optimizations.
asi.bottleneck: Build/store creation dominates the workload; visible-first read is already effectively free.
asi.truth_check: Use profile:demo to verify any large benchmark win still improves the same end-to-end work.
asi.guardrail: Watch mutation latency periodically so first-render wins do not come from destroying interactive update performance.

description: Open-expansion visible-count initialization fast path improved the primary benchmark, but checks failed because the new prepared-input test fixture was accidentally unsorted.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping the generic full-tree recompute for the common all-open startup path should reduce build time because the builder already finalized subtree counts.
asi.observed_result: ~5.7% faster primary metric before revert, almost entirely from build time.
asi.rollback_reason: checks_failed: the optimization likely works, but the added regression test used a non-presorted fixture with preparePresortedInput() and failed before the candidate could be kept.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the open-startup visible-count fast path, fix the test fixture ordering (or build the prepared input with prepareInput instead), then rerun benchmark + checks and validate with profile:demo.
asi.learning: The dominant startup cost is still count initialization after builder.finish(); a targeted open-expansion initialization path is promising.
